title: GNU Make
description: Build automation tool that uses Makefiles to define tasks, dependencies, and recipes.

entries.label: Run a specific target
entries.description: Executes the recipe for the given target. Runs first target if none specified.

entries.label: Dry run
entries.description: Shows what commands would run without executing them. Great for debugging.

entries.label: Parallel execution
entries.description: Runs up to 4 independent jobs in parallel. Speeds up builds with independent targets.

entries.label: Override variables
entries.description: Passes or overrides variables from the command line. Overrides Makefile defaults.

entries.label: Conditional execution
entries.description: Conditionally sets variables based on conditions. Supports ifeq, ifdef, ifndef.

entries.label: Declare phony targets
entries.description: Marks targets as phony so Make skips filesystem checks. Always re-runs the recipe.

entries.label: Wildcard file matching
entries.description: Expands to matching filenames at parse time. Useful for dynamic source lists.

entries.label: Automatic variables
entries.description: $@ is the target, $< is the first prerequisite, $^ is all prerequisites.

entries.label: Include other makefiles
entries.description: Imports variables and targets from other files. Missing files use -include to skip.

entries.label: Debug makefile
entries.description: Prints detailed debug output showing why each target is (re)built or skipped.
